message:
feat(ft-xbnl0.2.3): cx-first StorageHandle::new mass migration in mcp_tools (tick 303)

Migrates all 8 production `StorageHandle::new` call sites in
`frankenterm-core/mcp_tools.rs` to `new_with_cx`:

- L166 `load_distributed_remote_panes` — scratch-handle for MCP
  distributed pane lookup (+ follow-on `get_panes_with_cx`)
- L948 `get_text` MCP tool (inside block_on)
- L1364 `search` MCP tool (inside block_on)
- L1646 `events` MCP tool (inside block_on)
- L1800 `pane_state` MCP tool (inside block_on; reuses tick-261 wezterm_cx)
- L2035 `workflow_run` MCP tool (inside block_on)
- L2843 `reservation_list` MCP tool (+ follow-on
  `list_active_reservations_with_cx` migration)
- L2949 `reserve` MCP tool (inside block_on)
- L3073 `release` MCP tool (inside block_on)

All sites resolve cx via `Cx::current().unwrap_or_else(for_request)`
with distinct binding names (mcp_panes_cx, open_cx, search_open_cx,
events_open_cx, wezterm_cx, wf_open_cx, res_cx, reserve_cx, release_cx).

The `block_on` wrapper in each MCP tool spawns a fresh CompatRuntime
per request; the `Cx::current()` resolve inside picks up any Cx active
on that thread (or falls back to a fresh `for_request`). For a caller
that threads Cx through the MCP dispatch loop, cancel propagates
through both the storage open AND the subsequent ops.

Combined with tick 258 (mcp_tools.rs 8 other storage/wezterm sites)
and tick 261 (MCP wezterm get_pane), the entire MCP tool surface is
now cx-threaded for every storage + wezterm op.

Verification: `cargo check -p frankenterm --bin ft` compiles clean
(5 warnings baseline).

Cumulative ft-xbnl0.2.3 ticks this session: 117 (187-303).
frankenterm-core internal sites: 66 (prior 58 + mcp_tools 8).
main.rs cross-crate total: 236 sites (unchanged this tick).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
